name: GeForce GTX 1080
description: Launch-day gamer darling; sips power compared to modern hogs, still smug about its 8 GB of GDDR5X.

name: GeForce RTX 3080
description: Turns electricity directly into heat and imaginary coins; may cause spontaneous PSU existential crises.

name: GeForce RTX 3090
description: Triple-slot monster; sounds like a leaf blower, warms a small apartment while mining regrets.

name: Radeon RX 580
description: Former mining farm workhorse; smells faintly of stale thermal paste and broken promises.

name: Radeon RX 5700 XT
description: Overclock it for +10% hashrate or +100% fan noise; whichever comes first.

name: GeForce GTX 1060 6GB
description: Budget hero of 2016; still found in gamer rigs and very optimistic mining guides.

name: GeForce GTX 1070
description: Efficient little miner; hoarded in 2017, forgotten in 2023, reborn as dumpster treasure.

name: GeForce GTX 1660 Super
description: No RT, no Tensor, no nonsense; quietly prints coins while newer cards chase ray-traced reflections.

name: Radeon RX Vega 64
description: Runs hot loud and slightly angry; comes with free central heating for your server rack.

name: GeForce RTX 2060
description: Entry-level ray tracer; insists it's 'future proof' while wheezing through modern workloads.

name: GeForce RTX 3070
description: All the hashrate of a 2080 Ti without the prestige; perfect for a mid-tier junkyard rig.

name: GeForce RTX 3060 Ti
description: Silicon sweet spot; miners loved it, gamers hated that miners loved it.

name: Radeon RX 470
description: Once lived 24/7 in a smoky warehouse; now retired to your trash-to-treasure mining contraption.

name: GeForce GTX 970
description: Advertised 4 GB, actually 3.5 GB plus a grumpy annex; still good enough for budget coin dust.

name: GeForce GTX 780 Ti
description: Kepler hotrod; burns watts like it's 2013 and nobody pays the power bill.

name: Radeon R9 290X
description: Reference cooler doubles as a space heater; fans scream in GCN dialect when under load.

name: GeForce GTX 460
description: Fermi relic; produces more nostalgia than hashes but still tries its little 40 nm heart out.

name: Radeon HD 7970
description: First 28 nm beast; early crypto war criminal responsible for many melted PCIe power cables.

name: GeForce 8800 GTX
description: The original GPU rock star; old enough to vote still hot enough to char the PCB.

name: GeForce 4 Ti 4600
description: Prefers rendering DirectX 8 shaders to mining; sulks if asked to do anything blockchain-related.

name: Radeon 9700 Pro
description: Ancient yet proud; produces so few hashes they might be considered collectible.

name: Voodoo2 12MB
description: 3dfx shrine piece; cannot mine but increases your retro cred hashrate dramatically.

name: Matrox G400
description: Beautiful 2D terrible miner; insists on perfect signal quality while grinding out single-digit hashes.

name: Intel Arc A770
description: New kid with quirky drivers; occasionally forgets it's mining and decides to recompile its shaders instead.

name: GeForce RTX 4090
description: Converts kilowatts directly into bragging rights; might bend your case, your rails, and local building codes.

name: Radeon RX 7900 XTX
description: Chiplet chaos engine; mines hard, games harder, occasionally attempts to escape its own cooler.

name: GeForce RTX 2080 Ti
description: Once a flagship now a very fancy space heater with delusions of grandeur.

name: Titan V
description: Volta-powered research card; horrifically expensive, suspiciously comfortable living in a dusty lab rack.

name: GeForce GTX 750 Ti
description: Early Maxwell efficiency demo; perfect for ultra-budget rigs and very patient miners.

name: Radeon HD 5850
description: Prehistoric mining hero; still tastes faintly of 2011 Bitcoin mania and cheap PSUs.
